 {"id":"wa-nu4.2.3.4","title":"Extend browser smoke-test/doctor checks to Anthropic+Google","description":"# Task: Extend browser smoke-test/doctor checks to Anthropic + Google

## Goal
Make it easy to validate browser automation health per service/account without running a full workflow.

## Design
Extend the existing browser smoke-test command (Phase 2) so it supports:
- `--service anthropic`
- `--service google`
- optional `--account <key>`

Command behavior:
- Non-destructive: only opens pages and checks auth state.
- Clear exit codes and JSON output in robot mode.
- Human mode uses rich output (Phase 4), but v0.1 can start with plain text.

## Outcome taxonomy (must align with auth realities matrix)
Use the same canonical outcomes as `wa-nu4.2.3.5`:
- `Automated`
- `NeedsHuman`
- `Fail`

If the CLI chooses user-friendly labels (e.g., Success/NeedsHuman/Failed), it **must** also emit the canonical outcome field for parity.

## What to test
- Profile directory exists/created.
- Playwright initialization works.
- Service landing page reachable.
- Auth state detection works:
  - signed in → `Automated`
  - signed out/MFA → `NeedsHuman`
  - selector drift or navigation failure → `Fail`

## Deliverables
- CLI entrypoint (or `wa doctor --browser`) that runs these checks.
- Structured error mapping so workflows can call the same code paths.
- Outcome mapping consistent with the auth realities matrix.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Running smoke-test for each service produces one of:
  - `Automated` (already logged in)
  - `NeedsHuman` (with next steps)
  - `Fail` (with actionable message)
- Outcomes are consistent with the matrix (no ad-hoc state names).


## Testing
- Manual test matrix:
  - Run per service with an already-authenticated profile and verify `Automated`.
  - Run with a fresh profile and verify `NeedsHuman` (not `Fail`).

- Artifact/redaction checks:
  - Ensure any captured screenshots/logs redact or avoid tokenized URLs.

 {"id":"wa-nu4.2.3.5","title":"Auth realities matrix — Anthropic + Google","description":"# Task: Auth realities matrix — Anthropic + Google

## Goal
Extend the auth realities matrix to **Anthropic/Claude Code** and **Google/Gemini**, establishing which states are automatable and which require human bootstrap.

This completes PLAN.md Open Question #3 across all services and makes workflow decisions deterministic.

## Scope
- Anthropic `/login` flow
- Google/Gemini `/auth` flow
- Manual exploration of real-world auth states and detection signals
- Maintain the **same outcome taxonomy** as OpenAI:
  - `Automated`
  - `NeedsHuman`
  - `Fail`

## Deliverables
1. **Cross-service auth realities matrix** (single table including OpenAI, Anthropic, Google):
   - state name
   - detection signals (selectors / URLs / CLI prompts)
   - automation outcome
   - required human steps
   - safe retry guidance

2. **Workflow guidance updates**:
   - consistent “next steps” messaging for `NeedsHuman` cases
   - service-specific nuances documented

3. **Redaction rules** for auth artifacts:
   - scrub tokenized URLs, session cookies, device codes

> Note: Smoke-test alignment against this matrix is tracked in `wa-nu4.2.3.4`.

## Testing / validation (manual)
- Run manual auth checks per service in controlled scenarios:
  - already-authenticated profile → `Automated`
  - fresh profile → `NeedsHuman`
  - forced MFA/SSO (if possible) → `NeedsHuman`

- Redaction checks:
  - notes/artifacts must not contain tokenized URLs or session cookies

## Acceptance Criteria
- The matrix includes **all three services** and clearly states automation feasibility.
- Each service has at least one real-world validation run captured in the decision notes.
- Outcome taxonomy and fallback messaging are consistent across services.

 {"id":"wa-nu4.2.4","title":"[EPIC] Cass integration for Claude session correlation + token accounting","description":"# Cass integration

## Goal
Integrate `cass` so wa can correlate Claude sessions when no session id is printed.

## Deliverables
- wrappers around `cass query/search`
- correlation heuristics (cwd + start time + banner match)
- store external_id in agent_sessions



## Success Criteria
- Claude session correlation and token accounting integrate via cass without breaking core capture/index flows.
- Token usage summaries are persisted and exportable.
- Tests cover parsing/correlation logic and do not require network access.


## Testing
- Fixture-driven tests:
  - Use a fake/stub cass executable to return fixture JSON.
  - Cover:
    - successful correlation
    - ambiguous correlation (multiple candidates)
    - missing cass binary / non-zero exit

- Safety/UX:
  - Ensure ambiguity produces a stable error code and actionable guidance (not a silent wrong match).

## Acceptance Criteria
- All items in the following sections are satisfied: Success Criteria, Deliverables.
- All Testing requirements pass (unit/integration/E2E) with the logging/artifacts described above.
- Any explicit dependencies for this bead are implemented and validated via tests or E2E artifacts.

-{"id":"wa-nu4.2.4.1","title":"Implement cass CLI wrapper (query/search JSON, timeouts, errors)","description":"# Task: Implement cass CLI wrapper

## Goal
Treat `cass` (coding_agent_session_search) as an external "truth source" and expose a small internal API for:
- querying a known session id
- searching sessions by path/agent
- extracting useful summary fields (token counts, timestamps)

## Why
Claude Code may not expose stable terminal session identifiers, and token accounting is frequently only available out-of-band.
`cass` already has domain logic and history storage; wa should reuse it rather than reinvent it.

## Requirements
- Robust process execution:
  - handle `cass` not installed (actionable error)
  - handle non-zero exit codes
  - enforce timeouts (avoid hanging workflows)
- JSON parsing with serde:
  - version-tolerant (ignore unknown fields)
  - clear error mapping for invalid JSON
- Safety:
  - never log full transcripts by default
  - avoid storing raw message content unless explicitly requested

## Deliverables
- `cass::query(session_id)` and `cass::search(path, agent)` wrappers.
- Data models for the JSON we rely on.
- Error enum that distinguishes: missing tool / timeout / parse error / cass returned no match.

## Testing
- Covered by `wa-nu4.2.4.4` (fixtures + missing-tool + ambiguity cases).

## Acceptance Criteria
- Given a captured JSON fixture, parsing succeeds.
- When `cass` binary is missing, wa produces a clear error + next steps.

 {"id":"wa-nu4.2.4.2","title":"Cass session correlation: choose stable session key + persist external_id","description":"# Task: Cass session correlation (wa session ↔ cass session)

## Goal
Reliably correlate a wa-observed agent session (pane_id + timestamps + cwd) with a `cass` session record, and persist the linkage in wa's DB (`agent_sessions.external_id` or similar).

## Why
We need a stable way to answer:
- "Which cass transcript corresponds to this pane/session?"
- "How many tokens/messages have accumulated for this session?"

This is especially important for Claude Code where the terminal output may not provide a robust session identifier.

## Constraints
- Correlation cannot depend on fragile UI strings.
- Correlation must tolerate:
  - multiple concurrent sessions in the same repo
  - restarts/reconnects
  - slightly skewed clocks

## Proposed correlation algorithm (v0.1)
1. Determine `project_path` (from pane cwd; normalize to repo root if possible).
2. Determine `agent_type` (Claude Code).
3. Compute `start_window`: `[session_started_at - 10m, session_started_at + 10m]`.
4. `cass search --path <project_path> --agent <agent>`.
5. Filter candidates by `started_at` in window.
6. If multiple candidates remain, break ties using:
   - most recent `started_at`
   - optional: presence of unique "first prompt" hash if we can capture one
7. Persist:
   - cass session id
   - correlation confidence score + algorithm version

## Manual override
Provide a way to manually set/override correlation (config or CLI) for debugging.

## Deliverables
- `SessionCorrelation` type with: chosen id, confidence, reasons.
- Storage persistence of external_id + metadata.
- A small, deterministic test corpus for ambiguous cases.

## Acceptance Criteria
- On a fixture set with 2+ candidate cass sessions, correlation deterministically selects the intended one.
- When no candidate is found, wa records "unlinked" state without blocking other workflows.


## Testing
- Fixture corpus requirements:
  - Multiple concurrent sessions in same path (ambiguity).
  - Clock-skew variant (cass start time slightly outside window).
  - “No candidate found” variant.

- Determinism:
  - Tests must assert the chosen external_id and confidence/reasons are stable.

 {"id":"wa-nu4.2.4.3","title":"Token accounting via cass: aggregate tokens/messages and persist session summary","description":"# Task: Token accounting via cass

## Goal
Once a wa session is correlated to a cass session, compute and persist a compact accounting summary:
- total tokens (and/or input/output if available)
- message count
- last message timestamp

This is used for:
- status displays
- postmortems
- deciding when to rotate accounts / pause automation

## Design
- Do not store full cass transcripts in wa DB by default.
- Persist only summary fields + external session id.
- Provide an explicit "refresh" path that re-queries cass when needed.

## Implementation outline
1. Load cass session via wrapper (`cass query`).
2. Compute summary:
   - `tokens_total = sum(message.token_count)` (skip missing)
   - `messages_total = messages.len()`
   - timestamps: min(started_at), max(last message ts)
3. Persist to `agent_sessions` row for the current wa session.
4. Cache last refresh time to avoid polling cass too often.

## Deliverables
- `CassSessionSummary` type.
- Storage call(s) to upsert token accounting into `agent_sessions`.
- Strategy for refresh triggers:
  - manual (`wa status --refresh-cass`)
  - workflow step (Claude usage limit)
  - periodic background refresh (optional)

## Testing
- Covered by `wa-nu4.2.4.4` (fixtures + missing fields + missing tool behavior).

## Acceptance Criteria
- Given a cass JSON fixture with token_count fields, summary matches expected totals.
- Missing token_count fields do not cause failure; totals are still computed.

 {"id":"wa-nu4.2.4.4","title":"Cass integration tests: fixtures, missing-tool behavior, ambiguity cases","description":"# Task: Cass integration tests

## Goal
Lock down cass integration behavior so it stays reliable as external tools evolve.

## Test categories
1. **Parsing fixtures**
   - `cass query` output → `CassSession` model
   - ensure unknown fields do not break parsing

2. **Missing tool**
   - if `cass` is not installed or not executable:
     - error is classified as `MissingTool`
     - workflow surfaces actionable next steps

3. **Ambiguous correlation**
   - multiple sessions in same path within time window
   - ensure deterministic tie-break

4. **Token accounting**
   - sum token_count fields correctly
   - tolerate missing token_count entries

## Deliverables
- A small fixture set committed in-tree.
- Unit tests for wrapper + correlation + aggregation.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Tests cover success + error cases.
- Lint passes (avoid flaky time-based tests; use fixed timestamps).


## Testing
- Meta-validation:
  - Ensure tests actually execute the code paths that spawn `cass` by using a stub binary and asserting it was invoked.
  - Assert stable error codes for MissingTool and AmbiguousCorrelation so higher layers can react deterministically.

 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.9.10","title":"Beads hygiene: eliminate bd lint template warnings (Acceptance Criteria headings, missing sections)","description":"# Task: Beads hygiene (template lint to zero)

## Goal
Make the Beads plan itself reliable and self-documenting by ensuring bd lint produces zero template warnings.

This improves:
- onboarding for future contributors
- clarity during implementation
- confidence that tasks have explicit acceptance criteria

## Scope
- For all open tasks/features/bugs:
  - ensure the description includes the required section heading(s):
    - tasks/features: ## Acceptance Criteria
    - bugs: ## Steps to Reproduce, ## Acceptance Criteria
  - ensure the acceptance criteria are concrete and testable

## Change discipline
- Do not delete or truncate existing content.
- Prefer minimal edits:
  - rename "## Acceptance" -> "## Acceptance Criteria"
  - add a missing section at the end if absent

## Acceptance Criteria
- bd lint --status open produces no warnings.
- New issues created in this project follow the template by default.


## Testing
- Run `bd lint` locally after any template edits.
- Add a simple CI check (if desired) that runs `bd lint --status open` and fails on warnings.
","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T11:05:50.031963721Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-18T17:41:13.350674195Z","closed_at":"2026-01-18T17:41:13.350674195Z","close_reason":"Completed: bd lint now returns zero template warnings for all open issues (Acceptance Criteria + Testing sections present).","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["area-ci","area-docs","phase-4"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.9.10","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.3.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:07Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.9.2","title":"Docs: README/quickstart + architecture + CLI/robot/MCP reference","description":"# Task: Documentation for v0.1

## Goal
Make the project approachable for a new contributor and operable for users.

This bead is intentionally self-contained and mirrors PLAN Appendix A + F so contributors don’t need to dig through planning docs.

## Deliverables
### 1) README (operator-first)
- What wa is (WezTerm mux “terminal hypervisor” for agent fleets).
- Quickstart:
  - install wa
  - `wa setup`
  - `wa watch`
  - `wa status`
  - `wa query` / `wa events`
- Safety guarantees:
  - observe vs act split
  - no silent gaps
  - policy-gated sending + approvals

### 2) Architecture doc
- Ingest → Storage (SQLite+FTS5) → Pattern engine → Workflow engine → Robot/MCP.
- Deterministic state via OSC 133.
- Explicit GAP semantics.

### 3) CLI reference (PLAN Appendix A)
Human-oriented commands (rich output by default via `rich_rust`):
- `wa status` — panes + inferred agent state
- `wa watch` — run daemon (foreground/background)
- `wa stop` — stop watcher in workspace safely
- `wa events` — recent/unhandled events
- `wa query` — FTS search
- `wa send` — guarded send to a pane
- `wa reserve` — reserve a pane for exclusive use
- `wa reservations` — list active reservations
- `wa approve` — grant allow-once approvals
- `wa workflow` — run a workflow manually (guarded)
- `wa audit` — audit trail of actions (redacted)
- `wa history` — action timeline + undoability
- `wa undo` — undo supported actions (with confirmation)
- `wa rules` — list/test rule packs, show matching traces
- `wa accounts` — list/refresh accounts, show rotation picks
- `wa setup` — canonical WezTerm configuration (local/remote)
- `wa doctor` — environment checks (wezterm presence, DB health)
- `wa diag bundle` — sanitized diagnostic bundle export
- `wa export` — export slices/history/audit
- `wa web` — optional HTTP server (`fastapi_rust`) if enabled
- `wa tui` — optional TUI (`charmed_rust`) if enabled
- `wa sync` — optional sync (`asupersync`) if enabled

Robot mode commands (stable JSON envelope; token-efficient):
- `wa robot state [--domain <name>] [--agent <type>]`
- `wa robot get-text <pane_id> [--tail N] [--escapes]`
- `wa robot send <pane_id> "<text>" [--no-newline] [--wait-for "<pat>"] [--timeout-secs N]`
- `wa robot wait-for <pane_id> "<pat>" [--timeout-secs N]`
- `wa robot search "<fts query>" [--pane-id <id>] [--since <iso8601>] [--limit N]`
- `wa robot events [--unhandled] [--pane-id <id>] [--type <event>] [--limit N]`
- `wa robot workflow <name> <pane_id> [--force]`
- `wa robot reserve <pane_id> --ttl <secs> --reason <text>`
- `wa robot release <pane_id>`
- `wa robot reservations`
- `wa robot accounts [--service <openai|anthropic|google>]`
- `wa robot accounts refresh [--service <openai|anthropic|google>]`
- `wa robot rules list [--pack <name>]`
- `wa robot rules test "<text>" [--agent <type>]`
- `wa robot quick-start`

### 4) MCP reference (PLAN Appendix A)
Tools (names are short and obvious):
- `wa.state`
- `wa.get_text`
- `wa.send`
- `wa.wait_for`
- `wa.search`
- `wa.events`
- `wa.workflow_run`
- `wa.accounts`
- `wa.accounts_refresh`
- `wa.rules_list`
- `wa.rules_test`
- `wa.reserve`
- `wa.release`
- `wa.reservations`

Resources:
- `wa://panes`
- `wa://events`
- `wa://accounts`
- `wa://workflows`
- `wa://rules`
- `wa://reservations`

### 5) Library integration map (PLAN Appendix F)
| Library | Role in `wa` |
|---------|--------------|
| `cass` (`/dp/coding_agent_session_search`) | Correlation + session archaeology; used in status + workflows |
| `caut` (`/dp/coding_agent_usage_tracker`) | Usage truth + selection; used in accounts + `handle_usage_limits` |
| `rich_rust` | Human-first CLI output (tables/panels/highlight) |
| `charmed_rust` | Optional interactive TUI (pane picker, event feed, transcript viewer) |
| `fastmcp_rust` | MCP tool surface for agent control (mirrors robot mode) |
| `fastapi_rust` | Optional HTTP server for dashboards/webhooks (read-only first) |
| `asupersync` | Remote bootstrap/sync layer (configs, binaries, DB snapshots) |
| `playwright` | Automate device auth flows with persistent profiles |
| `ast-grep` | Structure-aware codebase scans in “unstick agent” workflows |

## Testing / doc correctness
Docs must not drift.

- The quickstart commands shown in README should be validated regularly:
  - either by referencing existing E2E scripts, or
  - by adding a small “docs smoke” CI step that runs the exact commands from the docs in a controlled environment (tracked by `wa-nu4.3.9.9`).

- Robot mode docs should match the schema contract:
  - reference the versioned JSON schemas and keep field names consistent.

## Acceptance Criteria
- A new engineer can get to a running watcher and see events with no oral tradition.
- CLI/robot/MCP references match implemented command surfaces and schemas.

 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.9.3","title":"Version/build metadata: `wa --version` shows semver + build info + enabled features","description":"# Task: Version/build metadata (`wa --version`)

## Goal
Make it easy to know exactly what build of wa is running when debugging bugs, comparing machines, or filing issues.

This becomes non-optional once we span multiple machines and (optionally) a vendored WezTerm build.

## Output contract
- `wa --version` prints a **single, stable** line by default (human-friendly, scriptable).
- `wa --version --verbose` prints a multi-line block with detailed metadata.
- Output must not require a TTY.

## Fields to include (when available)
- `wa` semver
- build timestamp (UTC)
- git commit hash (if built from a git checkout)
- rustc version
- target triple
- enabled cargo features (mcp/web/tui/sync/browser/vendored/etc.)

Vendored mode (PLAN Appendix H):
- when `--features vendored` is enabled, include:
  - vendored WezTerm commit hash (the pinned commit)
  - any compatibility/version string we can reliably extract

## Why this matters
When automation spans multiple machines, “what version is running?” is the first diagnostic question. Vendored mode adds an additional critical dimension (which WezTerm commit?).

## Testing
- Unit tests for `--version` output:
  - non-empty
  - contains semver
  - stable (no ANSI, no random ordering)

- Verbose output tests:
  - includes the detailed fields and feature list
  - when built with vendored feature (in CI matrix), includes vendored commit hash

## Acceptance Criteria
- `wa --version` output is stable and does not require a TTY.
- `wa --version --verbose` provides enough metadata to uniquely identify the build.
- Vendored builds surface vendored commit metadata deterministically.

-{"id":"wa-nu4.3.9.4","title":"Release packaging: reproducible builds + single-binary install path","description":"# Task: Release packaging

## Goal
Provide a simple, reliable way for users to install and update wa:
- build a single lean binary
- ship artifacts with enough metadata to debug
- keep installation friction low

## Deliverables
- Release build produces a single binary (`cargo build --release`).
- Provide at least one installation path:
  - downloadable release artifact (preferred)
  - optional `cargo install --path .` for devs

- Document:
  - minimum supported OSes
  - required external deps (e.g., WezTerm installed)
  - feature flags and what they enable

## Reproducibility & safety
- Aim for reproducible builds (same inputs → same hash) where feasible.
- Publish checksums for release artifacts.
- Never require users to run unsafe install commands.

## Testing
- Smoke tests for the released binary:
  - `wa --version --verbose`
  - `wa --help`
  - `wa doctor` (in a minimal environment)

- Feature-matrix build verification in CI (tracked in CI bead):
  - default features
  - `--features mcp`
  - `--features web`
  - `--features tui`
  - `--features sync`
  - `--features browser`
  - when vendored exists: `--features vendored`

## Acceptance Criteria
- A user can install and run `wa --help` without cloning the repo (via a release artifact).
- Release artifacts include build metadata (`wa --version --verbose` is meaningful).

 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.9.5","title":"Dogfooding + fixture capture: real WezTerm runs for compaction/usage/auth (all agents)","description":"# Task: Dogfooding + fixture capture

## Goal
Validate wa in real conditions and capture stable fixtures for regression tests.

## Scenarios to run
- Codex:
  - compaction detection + handle_compaction
  - usage limit path (if reachable) or simulated fixture
  - device auth flow (openai)
- Claude Code:
  - compaction detection + handle_compaction
  - usage limit banner detection
  - cass correlation sanity
- Gemini:
  - session summary parsing
  - auth flow presence

## Outputs
- Add captured transcripts to the corpus (sanitized).
- Record any new banners/prompts that require new patterns.

## Acceptance Criteria
- At least one end-to-end successful run of:
  - capture → detect → workflow → verify
in a real WezTerm pane.


## Testing
- Sanitization requirements:
  - Before committing any captured transcript into fixtures, scan for secrets and replace with placeholders.

- Regression integration:
  - Each captured transcript must be wired into the corpus runner with an expected detection JSON so drift becomes a failing test.

 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.9.6","title":"CI e2e job: run scripts with verbose logging, keep artifacts on failure","description":"# Task: CI e2e job

## Goal
Run end-to-end tests automatically when possible and make failures diagnosable.

## Design
- Add a CI job that:
  - installs/ensures WezTerm (if feasible) OR runs in an environment where WezTerm exists
  - runs `./scripts/e2e_test.sh --all --verbose`
  - captures artifacts directory
  - uploads artifacts on failure

## Failure UX
- CI output should point directly to the artifacts.
- The e2e script should print a concise summary at the end.

## Registry
- Not a case. CI job must execute registry cases (`./scripts/e2e_test.sh --all`) and retain artifacts.

## Logging
- Follow the standard E2E harness logging contract (`wa-4vx.10.6`) and runner (`wa-4vx.10.11`).
- Emit per-step start/end markers with timestamps + durations.
- Include case name and pane ids in every log line.
- Redact secrets (device codes, tokens) in logs and artifacts.

## Acceptance Criteria
- A failing e2e run produces an artifact bundle that includes wa logs + wezterm logs + exports.


## Testing
- CI self-check:
  - Add a mode (or a dedicated small case) that intentionally fails so we can verify artifact upload behavior.
  - Ensure the job prints the artifacts path/URL in the log summary.

 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.9.7","title":"CI e2e (extended): run approval allow-once scenario + keep artifacts","description":"# Task: CI extended e2e job

## Goal
Run the phase-4 "approval allow-once" E2E scenario in CI once the feature set is available.

This job specifically validates:
- RequireApproval UX payload
- wa approve command
- approval storage
- audited outcomes + redaction

## Behavior
- Execute:
  - ./scripts/e2e_test.sh --case approval-allow-once --verbose
- Always upload artifacts when failing.

## Registry
- Not a case. CI job extends the registry run with additional approval/allow-once scenarios.

## Logging
- Follow the standard E2E harness logging contract (`wa-4vx.10.6`) and runner (`wa-4vx.10.11`).
- Emit per-step start/end markers with timestamps + durations.
- Include case name and pane ids in every log line.
- Redact secrets (device codes, tokens) in logs and artifacts.

## Acceptance Criteria
- A CI failure includes enough artifacts to diagnose whether the bug is in:
  - policy decision
  - approval granting
  - retry behavior
  - audit/export


## Testing
- CI self-check:
  - Ensure the job logs include the artifacts upload location.
  - Consider a “known fail” dry run to verify artifact upload and summary formatting.
